folio.label: 117v
folio.l: Vn to this olde , blynde , worthy knygút
That he shal haue , a ©ein , his eyen sigút
And whan that his wyf , wold don hi~ velanye
Than shal he knowe , al hire harlotrye
Bothe in repreef of hire , and othere moo
¢ ñee shal q¶ Proserpyne , wyl ©e so
Now be my modres soule , sire I ©ow swere
That I shal ©euen hire , sufficeau~t answere
And alle wo~men after , for hire sake
That thougú they be , in ony gilt take
Witú face bold , they shuln he~ self excuse
And beren he~ doun , that wolden he~ accuse
For lakke of answere , noonö of he~ shal deyen
Al had a man , sen a thyngö , witú hise eyen
ñet shulnö we wo~men , visage it hardily
And wepe and swere , and chide sotelly
So that ©e men , shuln ben as lewed as gees

note.notetxt: 1031there is a cross in dry point in the laft margin.ODR

folio.l: What rekketú me , of ©oure auctorites
I wot wel , that this Iew , this Salamouö
Fond of vs wyues , fooles , manyouö
But thougú that he fond , no good wo~manö
ñet hatú there founden , many a nother manö
Wo~men ful trewe , ful goode and vertuous
Wytnesse of hem , that dwellen , in céstis hous
Witú martirdom , they preeued heere constaunce
The Romayns Iestes , eke , make remembraunce
Of many a verrey , trewe wyf , also
But sire be nat wrotú , al be it so
Thougú that he seide , he fond no trewe wo~man
I prey ©ow take this sentence , as I telle can
She ment thus , that in souäaigne bounte
Nis noon but god , neither he ne she
Ey for verrey god , that nys but one
What make ©e so meche , of Salamone
What thougú he mad a temple , goddis hous
What thougú he were riche , and glorious
So mad he a temple eke , of fals goddis
Who mygút don a thyngö , that more for boden is
Pardee , as fair as ©e , his name plaistre
He was a lecchour , and an Ydolatre
And in his elde , he verray god forsoke
And if that god ne hadde , as seitú the boke
Spared hi~ , for his fadres sake , he shulde
Haue lost his regne , rather than he wolde
I sette nougút , of al the velanye
